# solarsage

```go
import "github.com/shaobaobaoer/solarsage-mcp/pkg/solarsage"
```

Package solarsage provides a high\-level convenience API for astrology calculations. It accepts datetime strings \(ISO 8601\) instead of Julian Day numbers and uses sensible defaults for house systems, orbs, and planets.

This is the recommended entry point for applications that want a simple interface without managing low\-level configuration.

Package solarsage provides a high\-level convenience API for common astrology calculations. It wraps the lower\-level packages with sensible defaults, making typical operations simple one\-liners.

Initialize once with Init, then use the package\-level functions:

```
solarsage.Init("/path/to/ephe")
defer solarsage.Close()

chart, _ := solarsage.NatalChart(51.5, -0.1, "2000-01-01T12:00:00Z")
phase, _ := solarsage.MoonPhase("2000-01-01T12:00:00Z")
sr, _ := solarsage.SolarReturn(51.5, -0.1, "2000-01-01T12:00:00Z", 2025)
```

## Variables

<a name="DefaultPlanets"></a>DefaultPlanets is the standard set of 10 planets used when none specified.

```go
var DefaultPlanets = []models.PlanetID{
    models.PlanetSun, models.PlanetMoon, models.PlanetMercury,
    models.PlanetVenus, models.PlanetMars, models.PlanetJupiter,
    models.PlanetSaturn, models.PlanetUranus, models.PlanetNeptune,
    models.PlanetPluto,
}
```

<a name="Compatibility"></a>
## func [Compatibility](<https://github.com/shaobaobaoer/SolarSage/blob/main/pkg/solarsage/solarsage.go#L198>)

```go
func Compatibility(lat1, lon1 float64, datetime1 string, lat2, lon2 float64, datetime2 string) (*synastry.SynastryScore, error)
```

Compatibility calculates a synastry compatibility score between two people.

<details><summary>Example</summary>
<p>



```go
package main

import (
	"fmt"
	"os"

	"github.com/shaobaobaoer/solarsage-mcp/pkg/solarsage"
)

func main() {
	score, err := solarsage.Compatibility(
		51.5074, -0.1278, "1990-06-15T14:30:00Z",
		40.7128, -74.006, "1992-03-22T08:00:00Z",
	)
	if err != nil {
		fmt.Fprintln(os.Stderr, err)
		return
	}
	fmt.Printf("Compatibility: %.0f%%\n", score.Compatibility)
	// Output is dynamic
}
```

</p>
</details>

<a name="MoonPhase"></a>
## func [MoonPhase](<https://github.com/shaobaobaoer/SolarSage/blob/main/pkg/solarsage/solarsage.go#L176>)

```go
func MoonPhase(datetime string) (*lunar.PhaseInfo, error)
```

MoonPhase returns the lunar phase at a given datetime.

<details><summary>Example</summary>
<p>



```go
package main

import (
	"fmt"
	"os"

	"github.com/shaobaobaoer/solarsage-mcp/pkg/solarsage"
)

func main() {
	phase, err := solarsage.MoonPhase("2025-03-18T12:00:00Z")
	if err != nil {
		fmt.Fprintln(os.Stderr, err)
		return
	}
	fmt.Printf("%s (%.0f%% illuminated)\n", phase.PhaseName, phase.Illumination*100)
	// Output is dynamic
}
```

</p>
</details>

<a name="NatalChart"></a>
## func [NatalChart](<https://github.com/shaobaobaoer/SolarSage/blob/main/pkg/solarsage/solarsage.go#L72>)

```go
func NatalChart(lat, lon float64, datetime string) (*models.ChartInfo, error)
```

NatalChart calculates a natal chart from a datetime string and coordinates. Accepts ISO 8601 datetime \(e.g. "2000\-01\-01T12:00:00Z"\).

<details><summary>Example</summary>
<p>



```go
package main

import (
	"fmt"
	"os"

	"github.com/shaobaobaoer/solarsage-mcp/pkg/solarsage"
)

func main() {
	chart, err := solarsage.NatalChart(51.5074, -0.1278, "1990-06-15T14:30:00Z")
	if err != nil {
		fmt.Fprintln(os.Stderr, err)
		return
	}
	for _, p := range chart.Planets {
		fmt.Println(p)
	}
	// Output is dynamic based on ephemeris data
}
```

</p>
</details>

```go
func NavamsaChart(lat, lon float64, datetime string) (*divisional.DivisionalChart, error)
```

NavamsaChart calculates the Navamsa \(D9\) divisional chart. Uses Lahiri ayanamsa by default.

<a name="ParseDatetime"></a>
## func [ParseDatetime](<https://github.com/shaobaobaoer/SolarSage/blob/main/pkg/solarsage/solarsage.go#L597>)

```go
func ParseDatetime(s string) (float64, error)
```

ParseDatetime converts an ISO 8601 datetime string to Julian Day \(UT\). Timezone offsets are handled correctly \(converted to UT before JD conversion\).

Supported formats:

- "2000\-01\-01T12:00:00Z" \(UTC\)
- "2000\-01\-01T12:00:00\+08:00" \(with timezone offset\)
- "2000\-01\-01T12:00:00" \(assumed UTC\)
- "2000\-01\-01 12:00:00"
- "2000\-01\-01 12:00"
- "2000\-01\-01" \(midnight UTC\)

<details><summary>Example</summary>
<p>



```go
package main

import (
	"fmt"
	"os"

	"github.com/shaobaobaoer/solarsage-mcp/pkg/solarsage"
)

func main() {
	jd, err := solarsage.ParseDatetime("2000-01-01T12:00:00Z")
	if err != nil {
		fmt.Fprintln(os.Stderr, err)
		return
	}
	fmt.Printf("JD: %.1f\n", jd)
}
```

#### Output

```
JD: 2451545.0
```

</p>
</details>

<a name="ParseHouseSystem"></a>
## func [ParseHouseSystem](<https://github.com/shaobaobaoer/SolarSage/blob/main/pkg/solarsage/solarsage.go#L620>)

```go
func ParseHouseSystem(name string) (models.HouseSystem, error)
```

ParseHouseSystem converts a case\-insensitive house system name to HouseSystem. Accepts: "Placidus", "Koch", "Whole Sign", "WHOLE\_SIGN", "topocentric", etc.

<a name="ParsePlanet"></a>
## func [ParsePlanet](<https://github.com/shaobaobaoer/SolarSage/blob/main/pkg/solarsage/solarsage.go#L649>)

```go
func ParsePlanet(name string) (models.PlanetID, error)
```

ParsePlanet converts a case\-insensitive planet name to PlanetID. Accepts: "Sun", "sun", "SUN", "Moon", "moon", etc.

<details><summary>Example</summary>
<p>



```go
package main

import (
	"fmt"
	"os"

	"github.com/shaobaobaoer/solarsage-mcp/pkg/solarsage"
)

func main() {
	pid, err := solarsage.ParsePlanet("Venus")
	if err != nil {
		fmt.Fprintln(os.Stderr, err)
		return
	}
	fmt.Println(pid)
}
```

#### Output

```
VENUS
```

</p>
</details>

```go
package main

func main() {
	pos, err := solarsage.PlanetPosition("Venus", "2025-01-01T00:00:00Z")

		return

	fmt.Println(pos)

}
```
